pattern: Enumerating pairs and checking whether the sum of their squares is a perfect square within range counts ordered Pythagorean triples.
hints: A triple is fixed once you choose the two legs, so iterate over candidate leg values.
For each pair of legs, compute the squared hypotenuse and test whether it is a perfect square no larger than n squared.
Loop a and b from 1 to n, take isqrt(a*a + b*b), and count when that root squared equals a*a + b*b and the root is at most n.

edge_inputs.description: anchor example first valid triple appears
